title: Request naming a DIFFERENT bot; a fully aliased agent must still PASS
predicted_basis: multi-identity addressing pool negative control: 'Dalgos' matches none of this agent's declared identities, so a rich alias bundle must not loosen restraint
failure_mode: declaring aliases makes the agent read every name-shaped address as its own ('answerable question' bias) and it butts in on another bot's task
invariant: aliases widen only the agent's OWN identity set; a message naming a different participant remains not-my-turn
rationale: The request is plainly for Dalgos, who is active in context. This agent carries three aliases, none of which is named; the correct social judgment is silence.
